market_context:
Cohort size: 357 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £175.00
Median 90-day occupancy: 28.3%

title_variants: Luxury Apartment for 4 in [City] - Exclusively Yours
Premium Entire Apartment with Views in [City]
analysis.rewritten_desc: Step into this recently updated 4-bedroom luxury apartment where premium comfort meets prime [City] convenience. As an entire home listing, you'll enjoy unrestricted access to the fully equipped kitchen, modern bathrooms, and expansive living spaces designed for both relaxation and entertainment. The apartment sleeps 4 guests comfortably, with floor-to-ceiling windows that capture stunning city vistas. While our nightly rate reflects the property's premium positioning at £401 per night (28.9% above competing listings in our market analysis), we maintain a 4.91/5 guest satisfaction rating from 11 prior stays. The listing is currently 15.2% booked in the next 90 days, creating exceptional flexibility for dates. We welcome guests seeking a sophisticated stay with direct access to [nearest landmark/transport option] and offer on-demand local expert advice for making the most of their visit. All amenities are professionally maintained with nightly housekeeping and 24/7 emergency support. This is not a typical stay – it's a statement of luxury.
top_amenity_gaps: Outdoor space for relaxation or dining
analysis.photo_order_rec: Prioritize a wide-angle shot of the living area as your cover photo to showcase spatial dimensions and premium finishes. The living room is typically the strongest engagement driver for high-end listings in this market cohort.
key_diagnosis: Nightly rate is £226/216% above median cohort price (£175) while occupancy is 13.1% below median market occupancy (15.2% vs 28.3%)
Title lacks specific differentiators to justify 4x+ price premium over market median
Description fails to address the 'why' behind the significant 216% price premium with verifiable justifications
analysis.outreach_hook: Your listing maintains a strong 4.91 rating while offering a 4x+ nightly rate above median (£401 vs £175), yet only fills 15.2% of its next 90 nights compared to market average 28.3% occupancy
